title: Exploring Computer Science
description: The CS theory behind the production code you write.

items.title: Anatomy of an HTTP Request and Response
items.content_html: Methods, status codes, headers, and body — the four parts of every HTTP exchange and how they form the contract your API actually promises.

items.title: Authentication vs Authorization in APIs
items.content_html: Who you are vs what you're allowed to do — and crucially, where each check happens. The conceptual model behind API keys, tokens, and sessions.

items.title: Client and Server: The Request/Response Lifecycle
items.content_html: Trace exactly what happens between a request and a response — who holds state, who does the work, and why the front-end/back-end split feels muddy.

items.title: Why HTTP APIs Forget You: Statelessness
items.content_html: HTTP APIs don't remember your last request. Learn why statelessness exists, what it costs, and how sessions vs tokens rebuild the memory you lost.

items.title: What an API Actually Is
items.content_html: An API is a contract, not a tool. Learn what an API really is, why it's a boundary between systems, and what you're invoking every time you curl.

items.title: Lists as Recursive Structures
items.content_html: Understand how CS theory defines a list recursively using cons and null, why recursive list operations are natural, and what Python lists, Clojure, and functional languages have in common.

items.title: Regular Expressions
items.content_html: The complete practical reference for writing and reading regex in production code — character classes, quantifiers, groups, flags, and common pitfalls.

items.title: Type Systems Basics
items.content_html: Understand type systems from a CS theory perspective — what types really are, how type notation works, and why every strongly-typed language enforces the same rules.

items.title: Big-O Notation
items.content_html: Understand Big-O notation for software engineers. Learn how to identify O(n²), O(n log n), O(n) complexities and optimize your code for production.

items.title: Compilers vs. Interpreters
items.content_html: Understand what compilers and interpreters actually do — the CS theory behind how source code becomes running programs, and why it matters for how you write and debug code.

items.title: Computational Thinking
items.content_html: Master the mental toolkit of computer science: Decomposition, Pattern Recognition, Abstraction, and Algorithm Design.

items.title: How Parsers Work
items.content_html: From raw text to meaning: How parsers use grammars to understand code and data.

items.title: Recursion
items.content_html: Understand the CS theory behind recursive problem solving — base cases, recursive steps, the substitution model, and why recursive algorithms are the natural fit for self-similar problems.

items.title: Trees
items.content_html: Understand the CS theory behind trees — the data structure powering your file system, database indexes, DOM, compiler ASTs, and Git history.

items.title: Finite State Machines
items.content_html: Understanding Finite State Machines (FSMs), the elegant model behind traffic lights, game AI, and compilers.

items.title: Regular Expressions - The Formal Model
items.content_html: How regex engines compile patterns to automata, why backtracking causes production outages, and what regular expressions fundamentally cannot match.

items.title: What is Computer Science?
items.content_html: Computer science isn't about computers. It's the systematic study of what can be computed, how efficiently, and how to solve problems algorithmically.

items.title: Home
items.content_html: The CS theory behind the production code you write. Big-O, data structures, algorithms, and computational theory for back-end and platform engineers.
